title: excoecaria benthamiana
intro: Excoecaria benthamiana, also known as the poison tree or killer tree, is a plant species native to Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its toxic sap that contains a compound called phorbol ester. The medicinal properties of Excoecaria benthamiana lie in its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of using Excoecaria benthamiana include reducing pain and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antiviral and antifungal activities. Excoecaria benthamiana is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal use, but caution is advised due to its toxic nature, as it should only be handled and consumed under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
